name: Macadamia Rum Mousse Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 stick unsalted butter, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. For the crumb crust, combine the nuts, flour, salt, sugar, and cinnamon in a mixing bowl, and stir to mix well. Stir in the melted butter and continue stirring until the mixture has absorbed the butter. Break the mixture into even half-inch crumbs, rubbing with fingertips. Place half the crumb mixture into a 9 inch Pyrex pie pan and press with fingertips to line the pan evenly. Place the remaining crumb mixture into an even half-inch layer on a cookie sheet. Bake the crust and crumbs on the middle rack of the oven for about 20 minutes, until crisp and light golden in color. Cool the crust and crumbs on racks. For the Mousse Filling, whip the cream until it holds soft peaks and set aside in the refrigerator. Sprinkle gelatin over water in a small, heatproof bowl. Allow to soak 5 minutes, then place over a small pan of simmering water to melt while preparing the filling. When the gelatin is melted, remove from the pan and allow to cool. In the bowl of an electric mixer or another heatproof bowl, whisk egg yolks. Whisk in rum, then sugar. Place over a pan of gently simmering water and whisk constantly until thickened, about 3 minutes. If the yolk mixture becomes too hot, it may scramble. Remove the bowl from the water and beat by machine on medium speed until cooled to room temperature. Whisk in dissolved gelatin, then fold in whipped cream and chopped nuts. Pour filling into the cooled shell and smooth the top. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and chill until set at least 6 hours. To finish the pie, top with baked crumbs. Alternatively, whip optional cream, spread half on mousse and top with crumbs. Then pipe a border of rosettes of remaining cream around edge of pie with pastry bag fitted with star tube.
Yield: one 9 inch pie
